document.title: Guidelines for Settling Disputes on Analytical (Test) Results
document.label: CXG 70-2009 - Guidelines for Settling Disputes on Analytical (Test) Results

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: These guidelines provide guidance to governments on the procedures to resolve disputes which arise between food control authorities about the status of a food consignment 1, when the assessment based on test results made in the importing country disagrees with the assessment made by the exporting country on the same lot2. These guidelines only address disputes related to methods of analysis or laboratory performance and do not address questions of sampling. The procedure examines only the validity of the importing country’s results on which non-compliance is alleged. It is recognised that disputes may arise from other cause(s), which should also be investigated3. These guidelines do not cover microbiological test results.

These guidelines only address disputes related to methods of analysis or laboratory performance and do not address questions of sampling. The procedure examines only the validity of the importing country’s results on which non-compliance is alleged. It is recognised that disputes may arise from other cause(s), which should also be investigated3. These guidelines do not cover microbiological test results.

sections.title: PREREQUISITES/ASSUMPTIONS
sections.text: The procedure described in these guidelines is operable and effective only when the conditions listed below are met. Competent authorities should therefore ensure that these are satisfied wherever possible. These conditions are: • both countries agree on using this guideline; • laboratories4 comply with quality assurance provisions and with the Codex Guidelines for the Assessment of the Competence of Testing Laboratories Involved in the Import and the Export of Food (CAC-GL 27), and the laboratories have been designated by their respective Competent Authorities in both the importing and exporting countries; • at least one representative sample 5 from the same food lot has been taken by the Competent Authority at import in accordance with established sampling plans and/or good sampling practices, where applicable; this sample has been split into three essentially identical parts for the purposes of primary analysis and for confirmatory analysis (reserve samples) 6; the split reserve samples should be kept in a satisfactory condition for the appropriate length of time;

sections.title: ANALYSING RESERVE SAMPLE
sections.text: A reserve sample is analysed, subject to it being estab lished that sample integrity and the chain of custody have not been compromised and subject to agreemen t between the respective competent authorities on the following procedures for analysis of the sample(s): 1. the timeline, and the time of availability of the sample9; 2. the analysis of the reserve sample by either the importing country’s laboratory in the presence of an expert from the exporting country or a laboratory chosen by the exporting country; 3. the methods of analysis to be used by the laboratory. If the original test result of the importing country and the result of the reserve sa mple differ by less than the critical difference Δ  that would be expected from measurement uncertainty of the results (see Annex), the importing country’s original assessment of the lot shall stand, and the dispute is thus resolved. If the dispute still exists, the measures outlined in section 5 of this procedure, using arbitration by a third laboratory, should be applied.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=4
level=1
A reserve sample is analysed, subject to it being estab lished that sample integrity and the chain of custody have not been compromised and subject to agreemen t between the respective competent authorities on the following procedures for analysis of the sample(s): 1. the timeline, and the time of availability of the sample9; 2. the analysis of the reserve sample by either the importing country’s laboratory in the presence of an expert from the exporting country or a laboratory chosen by the exporting country; 3. the methods of analysis to be used by the laboratory. If the original test result of the importing country and the result of the reserve sa mple differ by less than the critical difference Δ  that would be expected from measurement uncertainty of the results (see Annex), the importing country’s original assessment of the lot shall stand, and the dispute is thus resolved. If the dispute still exists, the measures outlined in section 5 of this procedure, using arbitration by a third laboratory, should be applied.

If the original test result of the importing country and the result of the reserve sa mple differ by less than the critical difference Δ  that would be expected from measurement uncertainty of the results (see Annex), the importing country’s original assessment of the lot shall stand, and the dispute is thus resolved. If the dispute still exists, the measures outlined in section 5 of this procedure, using arbitration by a third laboratory, should be applied.

sections.title: ANALYSIS OF REMAINING RESERVE SAMPLE
sections.text: The remaining reserve sample should be analysed by  a suitably qualified laboratory agreed on by the two countries, and a final assessment of conformity is based on the results from this laboratory. Failing agreement on the choice of laboratory the competen t authority of the importing country can select a laboratory. The original result and th e result from the reserve sample tested  in the step outlined in section 4 are discarded. If possible this laboratory should be i ndependent of the laboratory or laboratories whose results were compared in the step in section 4. 9 The dispute shall be resolved within the shortest possibl e time, which should not adversely affect the quality of the commodity during storage, where appropriate.

sections.title: ANNEX
sections.text:
The critical difference Δ between the two results to be compared is 
2
2
2
1 U U+ = Δ  
Where U1 and U2 are the expanded measurement uncertainties of the two results. 

In case a set of samples is involved, a different formulation for the critical difference should be used.

sentences.original: The competent authorities have the option to agree on comparison of the background information of the analysis of the sample.

classification.sentence: See ANNEX to GUIDELINES FOR THE EXCHANGE OF INFORMATION BETWEEN COUNTRIES ON REJECTIONS OF IMPORTED FOOD (CAC/GL 25-1997): "Where imported food has been rejected on the basis of sampling and/or analysis in the importing country, details should be made available on request as to sampling and analytical methods and test results and the identity of the testing laboratory."

sentences.original: A reserve sample is analysed, subject to it being estab lished that sample integrity and the chain of custody have not been compromised and subject to agreemen t between the respective competent authorities on the following procedures for analysis of the sample(s): 1. the timeline, and the time of availability of the sample9; 2. the analysis of the reserve sample by either the importing country’s laboratory in the presence of an expert from the exporting country or a laboratory chosen by the exporting country; 3. the methods of analysis to be used by the laboratory.

classification.sentence: If the dispute still exists, the measures outlined in section 5 of this procedure, using arbitration by a third laboratory, should be applied.
classification.pattern: Conditional clause plus main clause with modal 'should'

components.obligation: measures in section 5 using arbitration by third lab should be applied

classification.justification: Contains clear obligation triggered by condition expressed via 'should'; structure typical of normative conditional mandates; partial template fit.
notes: Conditional procedural mandate.

sentences.original: The remaining reserve sample should be analysed by  a suitably qualified laboratory agreed on by the two countries, and a final assessment of conformity is based on the results from this laboratory.

classification.sentence: The dispute shall be resolved within the shortest possibl e time, which should not adversely affect the quality of the commodity during storage, where appropriate.
classification.pattern: Main obligation clause with modal auxiliary 'shall' followed by qualifying relative clause using 'which' and conditional phrase with 'where appropriate'

components.obligation: The dispute shall be resolved within the shortest possible time
components.qualification: should not adversely affect the quality of the commodity during storage, where appropriate

classification.justification: Sentence mandates an action (resolve dispute) using 'shall' but includes a qualifying condition about quality impact expressed through a relative clause and a conditional phrase 'where appropriate', forming a complex obligation with condition pattern not matching known templates.
notes: Complex combination of obligation with embedded condition and qualification

pages.title: GUIDELINES FOR SETTLING DISPUTES OVER ANALYTICAL (TEST) RESULTS (CAC/GL 70-2009)
pages.summary: Página com o título principal do documento e cabeçalho de página identificados. Dois nós estruturais de nível 1 foram detectados: '1. SCOPE' e '2. PREREQUISITES/ASSUMPTIONS'. O texto de cada seção foi corretamente associado ao respectivo nó, respeitando a numeração e hierarquia. A seção 2 continua para a próxima página, indicada por continues_on_next_page=true.

A página apresenta um único nó estrutural de nível 1 identificado: a seção 3, com seu título em caixa alta e numerado. O texto da seção foi corretamente associado, contendo o conteúdo detalhado e subsequente ao título. A seção continua da página anterior (is_continuation_from_previous_page=true) e prossegue para a próxima página (continues_on_next_page=true). O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e não há título principal visível nesta página.

A página contém duas seções principais de nível 1, numeradas 4 e 5, claramente identificadas pelos títulos em caixa alta e numerados. O texto foi corretamente associado a cada seção. Ambas as seções indicam continuidade, com a seção 4 continuando da página anterior e progredindo para a próxima e a seção 5 iniciando nesta página e também continuando para a próxima. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado, e não há título principal do documento visível nesta página.

A página apresenta um único título de nível 1, "ANNEX", em caixa alta, seguido do conteúdo explicativo sobre a diferença crítica Δ. O cabeçalho repetitivo foi identificado e excluído do texto. Não há indicação de continuidade da seção entre páginas.
